title: DHS shutdown hurts families' access to detention facilities, Democrat says
summary:
DHS shutdown hurts families' access to detention facilities, Democrat saystoggle caption Andrew Caballero-Reynolds/AFP via Getty ImagesStay up to date with our Politics newsletter, sent weekly.
Families are having an even harder time than usual talking to loved ones in immigration detention or finding out where they're located during the current Department of Homeland Security shutdown, a Texas Democrat says.

title: The largest federal workers union says 'untrained, armed' ICE agents should not replace TSA
summary:
America's largest federal employee union says ICE agents are unqualified to replace TSA officers at US airports.
White House Border Czar Tom Homan said Sunday that the administration was actively working on a plan to integrate ICE agents into airports.
On Sunday, Kelley said many TSA agents have continued to show up to work despite the lack of pay.
During an interview on Sunday, US Transportation Secretary Sean Duffy said TSA officers, whose salaries start around $40,000 annually, can't live on $0 paychecks.
The airport said it already has enough staff for roles the government suggested could be filled by ICE agents.

title: Travelers still dealing with long TSA wait times despite ICE deployment: Live updates
summary:
Immigration officers were stationed at airports across the United States again on Tuesday as part of an effort by the Trump administration to help alleviate long lines and severe delays caused by understaffing at security checkpoints.
President Trump announced on Saturday that he would reassign agents from Immigration and Customs Enforcement to support the TSA.
Wait times at Houston’s George Bush Intercontinental Airport reached 4 hours on Tuesday the airport closed all but two of its security checkpoints.
In Atlanta, where waits reportedly stretched to as long as six hours over the weekend, airport management advised travelers to allow at least four extra hours to account for delays at security.
On Sunday, Trump rejected a similar compromise, but the new revised plan may be “acceptable” to the White House, according to insider reports.

title: Migrants Stranded at Mexico’s Southern Border Plan ‘Genesis’ Caravan to the North
summary:
The report cites estimates of approximately 75 thousand foreign migrants stranded at Mexico’s southern border due to the hardening of immigration policies under the Trump administration.
Border Patrol apprehensions at the southwest border have dropped to less than 10,000 per month since President Trump’s inauguration.
During the last fiscal year of the Biden administration, 1,530,523 illegal aliens were apprehended by the Border Patrol, according to U.S. Customs and Border Protection.
Randy Clark is a 32-year veteran of the United States Border Patrol.
Before his retirement, he served as the Division Chief for Law Enforcement Operations, directing operations for nine Border Patrol Stations within the Del Rio, Texas, Sector.

title: Trump threatens to deploy ICE to airports amid Homeland Security shutdown
summary:
United States President Donald Trump has threatened to deploy federal immigration agents to the country’s airports to “do Security like no one has ever seen”.
“NO MORE WAITING, NO MORE GAMES!”Trump’s warnings on Saturday arrived on the five-week mark of a partial government shutdown that affects the Department of Homeland Security.
To force Democrats to vote for Homeland Security funding, Trump has threatened not to sign any legislation that Congress passes.
It is unclear, however, how ICE agents would improve current conditions at the airport, given that they do not have the same training as TSA agents.
Advertisement“I look forward to seeing ICE in action at our Airports,” Trump wrote in one of his posts.

title: Survey Shows Few Biden Migrants Were Asylum Seekers
summary:
A survey by pro-migration groups shows that only one out of every 27 border migrants claimed to meet the core requirement for winning political asylum in the United States.
The data undermine routine claims by progressives and journalists that illegal migrants are seeking deserved asylum.
Congress’s immigration laws bar economic migrants, and the asylum exception is granted to migrants fleeing persecution by their home governments.
Several thousand migrants died while trying to reach the welcome offered by Biden’s deputies, including his pro-migration border chief, Alejandro Mayorkas.
Some migrants self-deported from the United States as ICE implemented the President’s deportation promise and mandate.

title: Elon Musk offers to pay the salaries of TSA agents as Trump threatens to replace them with ICE
summary:
The world's richest man is offering to pay the salaries of TSA agents who have gone unpaid for weeks as the partial government shutdown drags on.
Many TSA agents have stopped showing up for work, causing hourslong lines at security at many airports across the country.
President Donald Trump, meanwhile, threatened on Saturday in a Truth Social post to replace TSA agents with ICE agents unless Congress can agree on a funding proposal.
While the partial shutdown began on January 30, TSA agents received their first $0 paycheck on March 13.
As many as 10% of all TSA agents skipped work this week, according to the DHS.

title: Trump deploys ICE agents to US airports amid staffing issues, delays
summary:
Democrats and other observers slam deployment of US immigration agents to airports as dangerous and source of tension.
The Reuters news agency reported on Monday that the Department of Homeland Security (DHS) confirmed it has begun deploying hundreds of Immigration and Customs Enforcement (ICE) agents to assist in security at airports facing significant staffing issues.
CNN also reported that at least four ICE agents were seen at Newark Liberty International Airport in New Jersey outside New York.
AdvertisementMany TSA agents have begun calling out from work, and more than 300 employees have quit since the shutdown began, according to DHS.
Speaking to reporters on Monday, Trump said he had requested that ICE agents deployed to US airports remove their face masks.

title: Transportation Secretary Duffy says ICE agents are trained and can assist TSA at airports
summary:
Duffy's statement comes after the president said ICE would go to airports MondayTransportation Secretary Duffy says ICE agents are trained and can assist TSA at airportsPeople wait in a TSA line at the John F. Kennedy International Airport on Sunday, March 22, 2026, in New York.
Transportation Secretary Sean Duffy said Sunday that Immigration and Customs Enforcement (ICE) agents are trained and can assist with airport security, following President Donald Trump's announcement that ICE would be sent to airports starting Monday.
But again, we have ICE agents who are trained and can provide assistance to agents."
ICE agents are supposed to be at 14 airports across the country, multiple sources familiar with the situation told.
The number of airports ICE agents could be at could change, as plans are still being ironed out, according to sources.

title: ICE officers will help TSA at airports, Trump administration says
summary:
ICE officers will deploy to American airports to assist with certain security protocols beginning Monday, the Trump administration confirmed.
But the decision to augment TSA officers with ICE personnel was sure to be highly controversial on Capitol Hill and beyond.
Speaking on CNN, Homan said ICE agents would likely focus on logistical roles at airports typically performed by TSA officers — not screening baggage or passengers.
Meanwhile, the union representing TSA officers immediately slammed the decision.
The Association of Flight Attendants also criticized the move and called on Congress to instead pass funding to pay TSA officers.

title: Video ICE officers arrive at airport TSA checkpoints after Trump order amid shutdown
summary: ICE officers arrive at airport TSA checkpoints after Trump order amid shutdown Former Homeland Security official Elizabeth Neumann discusses deploying ICE agents to airports nationwide during the ongoing government shutdown.
